description: A compact two-piece chest rig with plenty of webbing to strap MOLLE pouches onto.  Can be worn over plate carriers.

name.str: Tactical Tailor MAV 2 Piece chest rig

variants.description: A compact rig with two large webbing panels, locking with buckles in the middle.  For its size, it offers plenty of space for MOLLE pouches, though not as much as full-sized load-bearing vests.  It could, however, be comfortably worn with slick plate carriers that don't offer any webbing of their own; the rig could then be swiftly doffed without removing ballistic protection.  <variant:multicam>

name.str: Tactical Tailor MAV 2 Piece chest rig

variants.description: A compact rig with two large webbing panels, locking with buckles in the middle.  For its size, it offers plenty of space for MOLLE pouches, though not as much as full-sized load-bearing vests.  It could, however, be comfortably worn with slick plate carriers that don't offer any webbing of their own; the rig could then be swiftly doffed without removing ballistic protection.  <variant:coyote>

name.str: Tactical Tailor MAV 2 Piece chest rig

//weight: manufacturer states weight of the rig itself (straps + panel) to be 8.4 oz = 238.14 g

we add 5 g × (4 + 2) = 30 g for the 4 female buckles included with the rig kit (source for weight/dimensions) and the two other QASM (female) ones on the X-harness, as visible on pictures of the rig itself, presuming that their weight is not included

notes: depth accounts for both the backing fabric and the PALS loops
trim, therefore, accounts for not the whole panel being filled with PALS loops
depth accounts for both the backing fabric and the PALS loops
trim, therefore, accounts for not the whole panel being filled with PALS loops
the buckle dimensions also account for the fabric strap holding the buckle attached to the panels
the buckle dimensions also account for the fabric strap holding the buckle attached to the panels

//dimensions: multiplied by 135% to account for the the rest of the straps that are difficult to measure
//longest_side: assumes the length of a single PALS panel (8 columns × 1.5 in. = 12 in.), folded in half: 12 / 2 = 6 in. = 152.4 mm ≈ 152 mm

notes: said to span the entire size of the panel

//max_item_volume: based on the calculation of volume for a map of New York City subway
//max_item_length: while the pocket itself spans the entire length of the panel, the opening is roughly 3 in. shorter

notes: said to span the entire size of the panel

//max_item_volume: based on the calculation of volume for a map of New York City subway
//max_item_length: while the pocket itself spans the entire length of the panel, the opening is roughly 3 in. shorter

description: A thin nylon mesh vest with lots of webbing to strap MOLLE pouches to.  This model was issued to the U.S. army prior to the end of the world.

variants.description: Fighting Load Carrier vest, part of the MOLLE II modular system of soldier equipment.  Designed to be worn over the armor, it can be equipped with multiple MOLLE pouches and disposed of quickly, without the need to doff ballistic armor.  <variant:ocp>

Fighting Load Carrier vest, part of the MOLLE II modular system of soldier equipment.  Designed to be worn over the armor, it can be equipped with multiple MOLLE pouches and disposed of quickly, without the need to doff ballistic armor.  <variant:ucp>

Fighting Load Carrier vest, part of the MOLLE II modular system of soldier equipment.  Designed to be worn over the armor, it can be equipped with multiple MOLLE pouches and disposed of quickly, without the need to doff ballistic armor.  <variant:woodland>

Fighting Load Carrier vest, part of the MOLLE II modular system of soldier equipment.  Designed to be worn over the armor, it can be equipped with multiple MOLLE pouches and disposed of quickly, without the need to doff ballistic armor.  <variant:coyote>

notes: calculated based on size of PALS webbing in front of the pocket
added 0.5 in. to height based on extra fabric under the webbing

pocket_data.//max_item_length: based on height, rather than the larger length, because otherwise hard long items could be stored in it, which would likely make wearing the FLC very uncomfortable

notes: calculated based on size of PALS webbing in front of the pocket
added 0.5 in. to height based on extra fabric under the webbing

//notes: the weight for this vest doesn't appear to be available even on Amazon, so we use a similar vest from the same company
it may be slightly or significantly heavier, since the package weight on this Amazon listing states 3.65 lbs = 1655.6 g
on Condor's own website, the vest is advertised with a belt that has two extra pistol-mag pouches attached to it
it does not appear to come with the vest, since other sites show images of just the vest, with the belt straps empty
description: A nylon vest with a lot of pockets.  It has three rifle magazile pouches, three pistol magazine pouches, a small general pouch, and a pistol holster, which has a magazine pouch of its own.

variants.description: A sturdy tactical vest, mostly mesh in the back and mostly solid nylon in the front.  It features three pockets for a rifle magazine, three pockets for a pistol magazine (or a tool of similar size), and an extra small pocket on the chest.  It comes with a pistol holster in the cross draw position: at an angle, on the side opposite the dominant hand, to be drawn with it; the holster has a separate pocket for a magazine.  Hook-and-loop shoulder pads combined with generous side strap belts allow for a large degree of adjustment, making the vest fit for any size.  <variant:black>

A sturdy tactical vest, mostly mesh in the back and mostly solid nylon in the front.  It features three pockets for a rifle magazine, three pockets for a pistol magazine (or a tool of similar size), and an extra small pocket on the chest.  It comes with a pistol holster in the cross draw position: at an angle, on the side opposite the dominant hand, to be drawn with it; the holster has a separate pocket for a magazine.  Hook-and-loop shoulder pads combined with generous side strap belts allow for a large degree of adjustment, making the vest fit for any size.  <variant:coyote>

A sturdy tactical vest, mostly mesh in the back and mostly solid nylon in the front.  It features three pockets for a rifle magazine, three pockets for a pistol magazine (or a tool of similar size), and an extra small pocket on the chest.  It comes with a pistol holster in the cross draw position: at an angle, on the side opposite the dominant hand, to be drawn with it; the holster has a separate pocket for a magazine.  Hook-and-loop shoulder pads combined with generous side strap belts allow for a large degree of adjustment, making the vest fit for any size.  <variant:olive>

//: special variant for the Prepped Patriot profession
this cannot spawn in the wild
variants.description: A sturdy tactical vest, mostly mesh in the back and mostly solid nylon in the front.  It features three pockets for a rifle magazine, three pockets for a pistol magazine (or a tool of similar size), and an extra small pocket on the chest.  It comes with a pistol holster in the cross draw position: at an angle, on the side opposite the dominant hand, to be drawn with it; the holster has a separate pocket for a magazine.  Hook-and-loop shoulder pads combined with generous side strap belts allow for a large degree of adjustment, making the vest fit for any size.  This one has been custom-painted after the American flag.

notes: taken from the dimensions of the PBA concealable vest, since it appears to be similar-enough
thickness adjusted for the fact that this isn't a ballistic vest

//longest_side: no inherent longest side, because the vest appears to be easily-collapsible

//coverage: half of large SAPI plate coverage = 18%

armor.//coverage: half of large SAPI plate coverage ≈ 6%

notes: dimensions assumed from visual reference based on the width of the Velcro patch being (presumably) 1 in.

pocket_data.//max_contains_weight: weight of a loaded M9 (2.55 lbs = 1156.7 g) + 50 g
//max_contains_volume: added 50 ml to account for red dot sights
red dot sight volume based on Loadout models

//notes: looks like this rig could be combined with another for a larger pouch set
description: A tiny chest rig with four rifle magazine pouches, four pistol magazine pouches, and two elastic cords for holding utility items.  Exceptionally-convenient, if not the most comfortable.

variants.description: An ultra-minimal, ultra-compact rig, intended to be worn concealed under an item of casual outerwear.  It features four rifle magazine pockets, four pistol magazine pockets, and two retention cords for larger, utility items.  The rifle pockets are stretchy enough to accomodate larger items, such as smoke grenades.  <variant:multicam>

An ultra-minimal, ultra-compact rig, intended to be worn concealed under an item of casual outerwear. The rifle pockets are stretchy enough to accomodate larger items, such as smoke grenades.  <variant:coyote>

//volume: we use the dimensions of `pouch_magazine_556_condor` as the basis for this item's volume calculations
we modify the length by 2 in. to account for the shorter pouches on the rig
we also use fabric thickness, not pouch overall depth, for height
we then multiply this by 8 to get the approximate overall volume of the rig

//notes: while this pocket is intended for rifle magazines, it's been shown to hold other, larger items, such as smoke grenades
thus, we model these pockets as though they're able to hold those
pocket_data.//max_contains_volume: volume of M18 smoke grenade, rounded to the nearest 50/100 ml
pocket_data.//min_item_length: arbitrary, to prevent smaller items from being stuck inside
arbitrary, to prevent smaller items from being stuck inside
